schema.title: design concept / tunable
schema.anomaly_classes: subset of {compaction, export, flush, full_cpu, full_memory, network_bandwidth2}
schema.iotdb_subsystems: subset of {Flush, Compaction, MemTable, TsFile, WAL, Memory, Network, Compression, Query, Cache, Procedure, Schema, GC, Election}

tuples.title: Choose encoding by data type and pattern
tuples.symptom: Disk usage higher than expected; compression ratio file shows ratio_sum/memtable_flush_time below 5; bad fit between data type and encoding
tuples.root_cause: Encoding mismatch: e.g. PLAIN on monotonic INT64, RLE on continuously-changing floats, TS_2DIFF on float with high precision (rounds to 2 decimals by default)
fix: BOOLEAN → RLE (default)
INT32/INT64/TIMESTAMP/DATE monotonic → TS_2DIFF (default)
FLOAT/DOUBLE → GORILLA (default, lossless) or CHIMP
TEXT/STRING low cardinality → DICTIONARY (over PLAIN)
Bigger-than-expected files → audit timeseries with: SHOW TIMESERIES root.**  and look for ENCODING column

tuples.title: Choose compressor (LZ4 default; trade off speed vs ratio)
tuples.symptom: CPU saturated by compression during flush/compaction (high CPU even when write rate moderate); or disk usage high despite good encoding
tuples.root_cause: GZIP/LZMA2 = slow CPU heavy (high ratio); SNAPPY/LZ4 = fast (medium ratio); UNCOMPRESSED skips CPU but blows up disk
fix: Default: LZ4 (good balance)
CPU-bound + small disk pressure: keep LZ4 or SNAPPY
Disk-bound + CPU spare: try ZSTD or GZIP for higher ratio
Read average compression ratio from data/datanode/system/compression_ratio: ratio_sum ÷ memtable_flush_time

tuples.title: Time partition interval (default 7 days) — sizing per workload
tuples.symptom: Single time partition produces many tiny DataRegion files (interval too short); or cross-time queries scan one giant partition slowly (interval too long)
tuples.root_cause: TimePartitionInterval controls how data is split horizontally; too small → many empty/sparse partitions and metadata overhead; too large → one partition holds too much data, compaction backlog concentrated, queries can't time-prune effectively
fix: Default: 604800000 ms (7 days) — good for most workloads
High ingest rate (TB/day): shorten to 1 day to keep per-partition data bounded
Long-retention low-throughput: extend to 30 days to reduce partition count
Check current value via SHOW VARIABLES → TimePartitionInterval
tuples.version: all (set at cluster init; rarely hot-changeable)

tuples.title: SeriesSlotNum (default 1000) — series partition cardinality
tuples.symptom: Hot SchemaRegion / DataRegion (one slot holds disproportionate load); ConfigNode partition table memory bloats; uneven CPU across DataNodes
tuples.root_cause: Default hash partitioning splits timeseries into 1000 buckets; if data is heavily skewed in series namespace (e.g. one device with millions of metrics), buckets become uneven
fix: Default 1000 fits most cases (balances metadata size vs balance quality)
For highly skewed metric distributions: switch SeriesSlotExecutorClass to a custom hash or list partitioner
Use SHOW REGIONS + DiskSpaceWarningThreshold dashboards to detect imbalance

tuples.title: Replication factor and fault-tolerant Region placement
tuples.symptom: After a single DataNode failure, neighbour DataNode immediately overloaded (CPU/memory spike)
tuples.root_cause: Naive round-robin Region placement → failover concentrates load on one peer; IoTDB's fault-tolerant placement spreads replicas so failover load fans out
fix: DataReplicationFactor ≥ 2 for HA (cluster default 2)
SchemaReplicationFactor = 3 typical (RatisConsensus needs odd quorum)
Verify even Region distribution via SHOW REGIONS — leader count per node should be ≈ total_leaders / N
If skewed: MIGRATE REGION manually or wait for next Thursday 08:00 auto-rebalance

tuples.title: Leader balance algorithm — diagnose compute-skew across DataNodes
tuples.symptom: One DataNode persistently shows highest CPU / write latency; SHOW REGIONS shows it holding disproportionately many Leader roles
tuples.root_cause: Leader Region handles primary write/read load; greedy leader selection can produce a locally-optimal unbalanced layout — IoTDB uses global leader balance algorithm to mitigate, but new region additions may temporarily skew
fix: SHOW REGIONS — count Role=Leader per DataNode
Wait for next rebalance window (~Thursday 08:00) or
Manually MIGRATE REGION to move leader away
If skew persists, consider increasing DataRegionPerDataNode to give scheduler more flexibility

tuples.title: TTL-driven migration-free elastic storage
tuples.symptom: Old data not auto-deleted; disk fills despite TTL set; manual deletion needed
tuples.root_cause: IoTDB relies on TTL + time-partition expiration for automatic archival; if TTL not set or partition interval misconfigured, partitions linger
fix: SET TTL TO root.<db> <ms>  — set per-database TTL
SHOW TTL  — verify
After TTL elapses, expired time partitions are reclaimed during compaction; check SHOW DISK_USAGE drop
If still not freeing space, ensure compaction is running (file_count for relevant DataRegions decreasing)
